channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mideast mediator Oman at the center of a key first Iran-US meeting over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran and the United States will meet in Oman for the first talks for President Donald Trump’s second term over Tehran’s rapidly advancing nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israelis mark another Passover hoping for Gaza captives to be freed
item.description: NAHAL OZ, Israel (AP) — Relatives of hostages in Gaza say they feel the absence of their loved ones acutely during Passover, which commemorates the Exodus of the Israelites from Egypt and their liberation from slavery.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Western countries step up support for Ukraine as Russian and US envoys meet
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain on Friday announced a “surge” of military support to Ukraine, as the war-ravaged country’s Western backers gathered to drum up more weapons and ammunition to fight off Russia's invasion and a U.S. envoy flew to St. Petersburg amid ongoing ceasefire efforts.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's Xi tells Spanish PM that partnership needed, no mention of talks with US
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China calls on Western countries to work to support multilateralism and open cooperation, Chinese President Xi Jinping told Spain’s visiting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez on Friday, as Beijing woos allies for its escalating tariff fight with U.S. President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's army says it will fire air force reservists who condemned the war
item.description: Israel’s military said Friday it will fire air force reservists who signed a letter condemning the war in Gaza and accusing it of only serving political interests instead of bringing the hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meeting on shipping emissions could result in the world's first global carbon tax
item.description: Many of the world’s largest shipping nations will decide Friday whether to impose a tax on carbon emissions from ships.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Mcdermott And Sibi Arasu, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ousted South Korean President Yoon embraces supporters after leaving presidential residence
item.description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol left the presidential residence in Seoul on Friday for his private home, a week after the Constitutional Court removed him from office over his ill-fated imposition of martial law in December.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian opposition leader says his home was the target of an alleged bomb plot
item.description: Australian opposition leader Peter Dutton has confirmed that his family home was the target of an alleged bomb plot but says concerns for his personal safety did not restrict his election campaigning.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ksenia Karelina, a dual US-Russian national released by Moscow in prisoner swap, arrives in US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Russian-American woman freed by Moscow arrived back in the United States late Thursday as part of a prisoner swap completed as the two countries aim to repair ties. A plane carrying Ksenia Karelina landed around 11 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea establishes diplomatic relations with Syria, a longtime friend of rival North Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea has established diplomatic relations with Syria’s new Islamist government, Seoul’s foreign ministry said Friday, months after a rebel coalition ousted President Bashar Assad, who had maintained close ties with North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs lead to soaring tensions, risks for US, China
item.description: Economic tensions between the U.S. and China are soaring after President Trump raised tariffs on Chinese products to 125 percent on Wednesday and China retaliated by raising tariffs on U.S. exports to 84 percent.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: Yellen: Trump 'has taken a wrecking ball' to economy with trade war
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen, who served under former President Biden, said President Trump “has taken a wrecking ball” to the economy.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hawley calls on Zuckerberg to appear before Congress following whistleblower testimony
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) called on Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg to testify before Congress after a former Facebook employee alleged the company repeatedly undermined U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Mexican drug lord convicted in killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena is freed
item.description: Mexican drug lord Ernesto "Don Neto" Fonseca Carrillo was released from custody after being convicted in the 1985 killing of DEA agent Enrique ‘Kiki’ Camarena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Greenpeace UK leader arrested for targeting American embassy with ' blood-red dye’: report
item.description: Greenpeace’s U.K. leader and five other activists reportedly were arrested after tossing hundreds of liters of “blood-red dye" into a pond at the U.S. embassy in London.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China on increased Trump tariffs: US must show 'respect' for any talks
item.description: Leaders in Beijing say they will not buckle under President Trump's heightened tariffs on Chinese imports, signaling a willingness to escalate the trade war if potential negotiations are not done with "respect.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: American ballerina released from Russia in prisoner exchange: Rubio
item.description: Ksenia Karelina, a U.S.-Russian dual national who was detained last year on treason charges, was released from Russia in the latest prisoner exchange, according to the State Department. “American Ksenia Karelina is on a plane back home to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan's president targets China influence, kicks out pro-Beijing agitators amid rising tensions
item.description: Taiwanese President William Lai promises an uncompressing stance against both domestic and foreign pro-CCP influence operations amid rising tensions between Taipei and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Officials scramble to ID victims of Dominican nightclub roof collapse
item.description: Crews at the scene were still looking for victims and potential survivors, although no one has been found alive since Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike on Gaza apartment building kills at least 23, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli aircraft struck a residential block in war-ravaged northern Gaza on Wednesday, killing at least 23 people, health officials said, as the renewed fighting in the devastated Palestinian enclave showed no signs of slowing.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 18 dead, 120 injured in roof collapse at Dominican Republic nightclub
item.description: A roof collapse at Jet Set nightclub in the Dominican Republic killed 18 and injured 120. Rescue teams are searching for survivors, while the cause remains under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Chinese men captured in Ukraine, Zelenskyy claims
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for action in the U.S. and Europe after claiming to capture two Chinese nationals fighting in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels killed at least six people, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sick sea lions, other mammals stranded on beaches in Baja, Southern California
item.description: Officials are reporting an unusually high number of beached animals due "domoic acid or phytotoxin" in the water.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation
